prompt: Design an actor-based network service in Swift 6 that fetches user profiles from a REST API. The service should support concurrent requests, cancellation, and expose a clean protocol-based interface. Include proper error handling with typed throws and Sendable enforcement.

assertions.description: The service implementation uses an actor (not a class with locks) for managing mutable shared state such as caches or in-flight request tracking.
A protocol defines the service contract with async throwing methods, and the actor conforms to this protocol, enabling dependency injection and test doubles.
CancellationToken or Task.isCancelled is checked in long operations, and the service supports structured concurrency with TaskGroup or withThrowingTaskGroup for parallel requests.
Error handling uses typed throws (throws(NetworkError) or similar) with a specific error enum. All types crossing actor boundaries conform to Sendable.
The design uses AsyncSequence, AsyncStream, or async/await patterns correctly — no completion handlers or DispatchQueue usage in new code.

prompt: Refactor a SwiftUI shopping cart view that currently uses ObservableObject with @Published properties. Migrate it to use the @Observable macro. The cart should support adding/removing items, computing totals, and persisting state. Show the view, model, and how state flows between parent and child views.

assertions.description: The cart model uses the @Observable macro instead of ObservableObject/@Published, and properties are plain stored properties tracked automatically by the observation framework.
View-local state uses @State for the observable object or primitives, and shared state is passed via @Environment or explicit parameters — not via @StateObject or @ObservedObject.
View body properties are side-effect-free. Async work like persistence is triggered via .task modifiers or explicit method calls from button actions, not from computed body properties.
Parent views own state and pass data down to child views through initializer parameters or environment. Child views that need to mutate parent state use @Bindable or callback closures.
Cart items are modeled as structs (value types) with let properties where possible. The cart model class uses @Observable but item data structures prefer value semantics for Sendable safety.
